content:
Hi everyone! I'm just starting as a translator today. After reading the information throughout the channel, I have a small question before starting.

Should I do the translation through github or through uploading in https://discord.com/channels/1402537664619479100/1446619934565204231 ? Since I saw most of the guide prefer Github. But there is still a channel like the one I mentioned so I'm a bit confused <:AniAwkward:1140987658013769768>

Hiya! I'm the one currently managing [the Coordination Repo](https://github.com/omnichromia/morimens-volunteer-coordination) mentioned in <#1416615133479960647>, <#1419429157779280035> and <#1446619934565204231>. It was set up as a more streamlined and easier-to-understand way for volunteers to participate.

When this project started, we were effectively just given one massive .xlsx file with over a hundred thousand lines and very little organization, and the method of submitting changes was a bit clunky (its okay ace I know you do your best ily) so I took up the mantle of making things easier for folks by splitting up the "Source Sheet" into manageable chunks and setting up methods of keeping volunteers from stepping on each other's toes.

That eventually manifested into the Coordination Repo, which I chose to keep private for NDA concerns. I'm still working on the backend and I have a lot less time to participate than I did at the start but it all works just fine.
## It is *heavily* preferred that you use my repo if it has the content you're looking to work on, as it solves a lot of various problems the other system(s) have.

For clarification, all work for this project goes through "GitHub", though that means different things as GitHub is essentially just a server service not unlike Google Drive. There are two GitHub Repositories ("Repos");
[The official one is __BIAVGit__](https://github.com/BIAVGit/Morimens-Localizations/tree/main) and it's managed by Ace.
[My one is the __"Coordination Repo"__](https://github.com/omnichromia/morimens-volunteer-coordination) and if this link results in an Error 404, tell me what your GitHub username is and I'll give you an invite!
Hopefully that helps answer some questions, if there's anything more you'd like to know ask away!
ohhh that's really help! Thank you
and also my username is Anicillia! Please invite me
One more question I'm currently have is either work done on both Repos will be contributed to the same final place right?
Sent!

Contributions to the game are sent in the form of .csv (UTF-8) files uploaded to BIAVGit by means of Pull Requests. Ace then reviews the files before implementing the contributions into the game at regular intervals, usually major updates.

My repo is there to make the process of working on the text easier for volunteers. Any contributions made through my repo will be programmatically formatted cleanly into the way Ace likes them, and I upload them to BIAVGit with all the proper accreditations.

Basically:

Your Contribution -> BIAVGit -> Ace -> Morimens

Your Contribution -> My Repo -> Me -> BIAVGit *(-> Ace -> Morimens)*
ah I see I see that's make sense
Easier for new volunteers sounds great xD Let me work on it
Though I'm still figuring out using Git... first  time really using it tho xD
I have a really shitty and phenomenally long but kind of hilarious explanation I wrote up forever ago on how GitHub works if you want me to forward that to you 😆
(but really evereything you need to know is in <#1419429157779280035>)
aight if that is everything It will be fine for me
just finished some tutorial on ytb xD

yeahh, most tutorials on youtube are about Git (not GitHub) and are in programmer-speak, really the gist of it is that GitHub is kinda like Google Drive (in that it's functionally equivalent to a folder on a computer) except that it has version control (everything ever changed is in a big ledger).
i think i get the hang of it
will push the file for you soon (as my first work ever done on Git lol)
also are there any tips to identify which part of the text is appear ingame?
for example this one in the Characters file
there's a hotkey you can set in the configuration which toggles ingame text into an identifier field
if you do that you can check fields ingame and look up the field to see which lines match it

No concrete way of determining that, but the repo is set up in the most straightforward way I could;

For example, you found that file in /**Archives**/Characters, so this part of the text is the Characters section of the Archives, also known as the Collection Hall.
ohh yeah!
so using gamer sense huh I see

But as Lens said there is a way to determine the inverse, but it wouldn't help a lot with finding things in my repo specifically.

There's a setting on PC that has to be manually keybound that replaces most onscreen text with the corresponding ShortKey for quick lookup in the Source Sheet file!
mhm I see I see

Hmm there are some  "\n" in those cells. I supposed they are meant to do the Line break right? 

I'm thinking of removing it. Should I? Because I found it a bit not really neat ingame. However I thought they might used it maybe for some reason?
Probably better not to mess with it, I assume it's there to fix some bug or other
yeah make sense after all
Yep! `\n` is a newline character. Use it or remove it as you see fit.
This just answer everything I've confused myself this afternoon
my god bless you

Quick reminder to check out the official style guide:
https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1gauwAJBvSQt8p12c39taKl4ubRzayxdccWH7VfYw3e4/edit?usp=drivesdk
oh
菲林 is an old way to say “film”, and then “詩社” means a club which is for discussing poetry. 社 for 社團. So it would be like a poet club named “film”
it seems like... xD
but okok i think i got the hang of this
Oof. For now here's some screenshots of the relevant parts
so peak...

Specifically, 菲林 means the black photo film (底片) 🎞️, not a movie 🎬

I know this topic was like 2 months ago tho… sorry to reply to this only now 😶‍🌫️ but I like the original text
I'll make the appropriate changes when I have the chance! Thank you so much <:celeste_pray:1408324281624170566>

I’m not sure if there is a better way to describe so I’m just trying… 菲林 is an old way to say film, meaning we don’t do this anymore. Nowadays we use another word to say film, and when we use “菲林”, it doesn’t always mean the daily life stuff “film”,  but more like an old fashion thing of vocabulary… 菲林 “reads” like film in some Chinese back then. This word seems still used as a daily life vocabulary in HK nowadays, just not so often in other Chinese speaking areas. So I guess, from some aspect, it would be more like saying “thou” instead of “you”. So it can be “a name” for a poet club/salon… like, a name, with a special taste. It’s just that I don’t know if there is a word to show the difference between saying film (2026) and saying film (1926)—when the game seems to be cannon (1920, Arc 2 Chapter 4, I remember). Also, the reason why I like the original text is because they chose the word for film (1926).

Additionally, the word “詩社” was also more commonly used back then—1920s. We don’t really use this word for any literature community as we used to do decades ago. (Or maybe it’s simply we don’t have so many 詩社 in the past decades, thus we don’t use and see other people using this word). So, the name “菲林詩社” itself contains the taste and the feeling of the era: could be something happened around 1920s, or something happens in 2020s which wants to be felt old fashioned.

The only one thing I don’t know is that… why do they, a poet club, provide “a special film”… like, they are poetry club, not photography club or movie club. But maybe they do more types of art… like, more than only literature. But, but, if it didn’t say that they provided “film”, I wouldn’t be sure that this 菲林 here means film, because this is a homophonic translation. In the pure Chinese, 菲林 is not *a* word, just looks more like a name with some good smelling and trees, probably a foreign girl’s name, but can also be a native girl’s name or even a boy’s name (depends on which century is it).
